name: Kintoan Barna Trail
name_original: Ruta Kintoan Barna – circular Eugi-Urkiaga (14 BTT Eremua)
description: Tackle the difficult 20.7-mile (33.3 km) Route Kintoan Barna circular Eugi-Urkiaga mountain bike trail in Monte Alduide, gaining 3401 feet.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Esteribar are set within the Navarrese Pyrenees, offering a diverse landscape for riders. The region features the ancient beech forests of Quinto Real in the north, the Arga River valley flowing through charming villages, and various Pyrenean peaks. The Espacio BTT y Trail EREMUA provides a dedicated network of trails, catering to different skill levels across its extensive routes. Best mountain bike trails around Esteribar The most popular mountain bike trail is Goitiko Gaina and Burdindogi Loop, a 15.4 miles (24.8 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through the Pyrenean landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Kintoan Barna Trail, a difficult 20.7 miles (33.3 km) path. This trail navigates through the Quinto Real forest, providing varied terrain and natural scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Saigots - Revirada – combinada circular desde Zubiri (10 & 11 BTT Eremua), a 15.3 miles (24.7 km) trail leading through the EREMUA network from Zubiri, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Mountain biking around Esteribar is defined by the beech forests of Quinto Real, the Arga River valley, and Pyrenean peaks. The extensive network of routes offers options for beginner, intermediate, and advanced ability levels. The routes in Esteribar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Esteribar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Esteribar?
A: Esteribar, particularly through the Espacio BTT y Trail EREMUA, offers an extensive network of 22 mountain biking routes, totaling approximately 230 kilometers. The komoot community has documented over 40 routes in the area.

Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Esteribar?
A: The trails in Esteribar cater to all skill levels, from beginners to advanced riders. The EREMUA space uses a color-coded system (green, blue, red, and black) to indicate varying distances and gradients. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes, with 3 easy, 18 moderate, and 21 difficult trails documented by komoot users.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easy mountain bike trails in Esteribar?
A: Yes, Esteribar offers easy trails suitable for families and beginners. The Espacio BTT y Trail EREMUA includes green-coded routes designed for less experienced riders. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed in the provided data, the presence of easy trails suggests options for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Where can I find facilities like parking, changing rooms, or bike cleaning for mountain biking in Esteribar?
A: The welcome hub for the Espacio BTT y Trail EREMUA, located in Zubiri (the administrative capital of the valley), provides comprehensive services. Riders can access information, GPS tracks, changing rooms, a public swimming pool, and bike cleaning facilities. Trailheads are conveniently located in both Zubiri and Eugi.

Q: What are some notable circular mountain bike routes in Esteribar?
A: Many routes in Esteribar are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989912782/saigots-revirada-combinada-circular-desde-zubiri-10-11-btt-eremua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saigots - Revirada – combinada circular desde Zubiri (10 & 11 BTT Eremua)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 24.7 km trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989924100/eugi-reservoir-mountain-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eugi Reservoir Mountain Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 28.3 km ride.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features will I encounter while mountain biking in Esteribar?
A: Esteribar offers diverse and contrasting landscapes. You'll ride through the majestic beech forests of Quinto Real in the north, home to centennial trees and wildlife. The Arga River shapes the valley, providing picturesque riverside scenery, charming villages, pine forests, meadows, and cultivated fields. You can also expect views of Pyrenean peaks like Adi and Saioa.

Q: Are there any interesting historical sites or attractions to see along the mountain bike trails in Esteribar?
A: Yes, Esteribar has a rich historical context. The ancient Royal Arms Factory of Eugi from the 19th century, with its rehabilitated ruins set within the beech forest, is a unique cultural element. Additionally, the historic Camino de Santiago pilgrimage route traverses a significant portion of the valley, offering cultural paths. You might also encounter sights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rabies Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4817230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magdalena Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I see any lakes or reservoirs while mountain biking in Esteribar?
A: Absolutely. The valley features the Eugi Reservoir, which is a prominent natural landmark. You can find routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989908548/zubiri-embalse-eugiko-urtegia-alternativa-circular-a-la-revirada-11-btt-eremua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zubiri - Embalse Eugiko urtegia – alternativa circular a la Revirada (11-BTT Eremua)&lt;/a&gt; that pass by it. You can also enjoy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Eugi reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3598622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ezkoriz Pond&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Esteribar?
A: Esteribar's diverse terrain makes it enjoyable across seasons, though spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in the valley, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potential snow in higher elevations, particularly in the Quinto Real forest.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Esteribar?
A: The mountain bike trails in Esteribar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Quinto Real forest, and the well-maintained network of trails provided by the EREMUA space.

Q: Are there any specific trails for advanced mountain bikers looking for a challenge?
A: Yes, Esteribar offers several challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988214960/goitiko-gaina-and-burdindogi-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goitiko Gaina and Burdindogi Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 24.8 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989934255/kintoan-barna-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kintoan Barna Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 33.3 km path through the Quinto Real forest.
tips: Link from the Lizartxipi pass to the Olabidea descent
Link from the ascent to the Adi from Urkiaga to the Senda de Zotalar.
A classic in the Eugi region for both road cycling and mtb. It is also the destination of the marked route 14 of the Eremua BTT known as "Kintoan Barna", a very cool and entertaining circular route.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Route Kintoan Barna – circular Eugi-Urkiaga (14 BTT Area)?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is approximately 33.3 kilometers (20.7 miles) long and typically takes around 3 hours and 42 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The Route Kintoan Barna is classified as difficult. While some sources describe it as intermediate, it features significant elevation changes, including over 1000 meters (3280 feet) of ascent and descent. Riders should have good physical fitness and be prepared for varied terrain, potentially steep gradients, and sections where pushing your bike might be necessary.

faqs.question: Where does the Route Kintoan Barna trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route starts at the Urquiaga Pass (Urkiagako Lepoa). Parking is generally available in the vicinity of the pass, which serves as a common starting point for trails in the area. You can also access the route from the village of Eugi.

faqs.question: Is the Route Kintoan Barna – circular Eugi-Urkiaga (14 BTT Area) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog policies for this particular BTT route is not readily available. However, as a general rule for mountain biking trails in natural areas, dogs are often permitted but should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. It's always best to check local regulations or the Eremua BTT network's official guidelines before heading out with your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike the Route Kintoan Barna is typically during the spring and autumn months. During these seasons, the weather is generally milder, and the natural scenery, especially the beech forests, is particularly vibrant. Summer can also be suitable, but be prepared for potentially higher temperatures, while winter may bring snow and ice, making the trail challenging or impassable.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to ride this trail or access Monte Alduide?
faqs.answer: The Route Kintoan Barna is part of the Eremua BTT Area within the Monte Alduide region. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on designated trails in this area. However, it's always advisable to check the official Eremua BTT network website or local tourism offices for any updated regulations or specific requirements for the Monte Alduide area before your visit.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Route Kintoan Barna?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse terrain, characteristic of the mountainous northern Basque Country. The route features beautiful paths surrounded by beech trees, and while some sections, like the descent towards Eugi, are described as a 'comfortable track,' other parts will involve steep gradients and varied surfaces. It rounds the Zotalar and the western slope of this hillside, offering a mix of ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several points of interest. You'll pass through the Urquiaga Pass (Urkiagako Lepoa) and navigate sections like the Olabidea Trail Junction and the Urkiaga Link - Zotalar Trail. The scenic beauty of the beech forests and the wider rural landscapes with mountains and valleys provide continuous visual appeal.

faqs.question: Does the Route Kintoan Barna intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Nafarroa Xtrem (29%) and also passes through parts of the Senda Pirenaica - E35 (9%), Euskal Herriko ibilaldia (6%), Leñariko bidea (7%), Zilbetiko bidea (4%), GR 11 (3%), and Karrobidea (2%).

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near the village of Eugi, where you can find facilities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. The wider Navarra region also offers various services for visitors. It's a good idea to check opening hours, especially during off-peak seasons.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification and significant elevation changes, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires a good level of physical fitness and mountain biking experience. There are parallel hiking paths in the area, and other BTT routes within the Eremua network might be more suitable for less experienced riders.
